questions.question: Why couldn't Bandar pull his hand out of the jar?
options: His hand was too full of nuts
questions.explanation: The text says: 'His hand was too full! He couldn't pull it out of the jar.'

questions.question: What does 'greedy' mean?
questions.explanation: The text says Bandar was greedy — 'he always wanted more and more food!'

questions.question: Bandar learned that being greedy makes you happy.
questions.explanation: The text says: 'He learned that being greedy doesn't make you happy!'

questions.question: Who owned the jar of nuts?
questions.explanation: The text says: 'said the baker who owned the jar.'

questions.question: Bandar stole more nuts after learning his lesson.
questions.explanation: The text says: 'He didn't steal anymore.'

questions.question: Match each body part with what we use it for.
Classify these body parts.
'Karen _____ like fruit salad.' Which word completes the sentence?
questions.explanation: For present tense with she/he/it, we use 'doesn't': 'Karen doesn't like fruit salad.'

questions.question: 'Bandar _____ let go of the candy.' Which word makes this past tense negative?
questions.explanation: For past tense negative, we use 'didn't': 'Bandar didn't let go of the candy.'

questions.question: Match each irregular verb with its past form.
Complete with the correct irregular past tense.
questions.sentence: I _____ in the park. My friend _____ down and hurt his knee.

questions.question: 'She _____ she was sorry.' Which word fits?
questions.explanation: 'Said' is the irregular past tense of 'say'.

questions.question: Complete with 'didn't' + the base form.
questions.sentence: Bandar _____ let go. He _____ steal anymore.

questions.question: We use 'didn't' + past tense verb for negative sentences about the past.
questions.explanation: We use 'didn't' + BASE form: 'He didn't eat' (not 'He didn't ate').

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: After 'didn't', use the base form: 'She didn't go home.'

questions.question: Put the words in order to make a sentence.
questions.answer: Bandar didn't let go of the nuts

questions.question: Put the words in order to make a question.
What lesson did Bandar learn?
options: Nuts are not good to eat
Being greedy doesn't make you happy
questions.explanation: The text says: 'He learned that being greedy doesn't make you happy!'

questions.question: Classify: regular or irregular past tense?
Match each word with its meaning.
Complete with the correct feeling word.
questions.sentence: Bandar was _____ because he couldn't play. He was _____ because he couldn't eat.

questions.question: 'The teacher _____ and helped him.' Which word fits?
questions.explanation: 'Came' is the irregular past tense of 'come'.

questions.question: A cook uses their hands to make food.
questions.explanation: The text says: 'A cook uses their hands to make food.'

questions.question: Put the words in order to make a sentence.
'I _____ my homework yesterday.' Which word fits?
questions.explanation: 'Did' is the irregular past tense of 'do': 'I did my homework.'

questions.question: Complete with the correct past form.
questions.sentence: The teacher _____ to help. She _____ kind words.

questions.question: 'Fell' is the past tense of 'fall'.
What did Bandar do at the end of the story?
options: He took just a few nuts and was happy
questions.explanation: The text says: 'He took just a few and was happy.'
